strategy: Attack with Myth, his lowest resist at 29%; avoid Life and Balance (69% resist) and expect 49% on most other schools.
Track every Krok Ankh. On Round 1 and every third Round after, he drops a random hanging effect (shield, trap, blade, heal-over-time, damage-over-time, or weakness) on himself or a wizard.
Clear every Ankh before the kill. If he dies while any Krok Ankh still hangs, he restores 100% of his Health, and that heal ignores Doom and Gloom and Infection, so you cannot cut it.
Bring the right removal for each Ankh: Shatter or Steal Ward for the shield, Cleanse Charm for the weakness, Triage for the DOT, and so on. Self-damage and plain attacks will not clear them.
Right before the kill Round, sweep all active Krok Ankhs, then hit through with your bladed Myth hitter.
At 32,085 Health this is a team fight: assign someone to track and clear Ankhs while the hitter sets up.
cheatNotes: Round 1 and every third Round he applies a random Krok Ankh hanging effect (shield, trap, blade, heal-over-time, damage-over-time, or weakness) on himself or a random wizard.

Each Krok Ankh must be removed with the specific spell or treasure card for that effect; for example the weakness Ankh will not clear from self-damage, and the shield Ankhs will not clear from damage.
Kill him while any Krok Ankh is still active and he restores 100% of his Health, so clear them all before the finishing hit.
That revive heal is a max-percentage heal, so Doom and Gloom and Infection cannot weaken it; clearing the Ankhs is the only counter.
